title: Dynamic Parameter Bindings
concept: The model generating argument values (like query, path, code) based on current environment state.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: why do we give each tool a clear name and description?

options.text: So the agent understands when and how to use the tool
To make the tool run faster
To hide the tool from the agent

exercises.explain: A tool's name and description act like a label on a button. They tell the agent what the tool does and when it makes sense to press it.

cards.title: Picking the tool isn't enough
cards.body: Choosing a 'search' tool is only step one. The agent still has to decide WHAT to type into it, like the exact words to search for right now.

cards.title: Dynamic parameter binding
cards.body: This is when the model fills in a tool's inputs (like a query, file path, or code) using what's happening right now. The values change based on the current situation.

cards.title: Filling out a form
cards.body: A tool is like a blank form: name, address, request. The agent reads the moment and writes the right words in each box, instead of using the same answers every time.

cards.title: See it in action
cards.body: You ask about the weather in Tokyo. The agent picks the weather tool AND fills the 'city' input with 'Tokyo'. Ask about Paris, and it writes 'Paris' instead.

cards.title: Not a fixed script
cards.body: Myth: the agent always sends the same inputs. Truth: it generates fresh argument values each time, shaped by your request and the current environment.

cards.title: Fixed vs. dynamic inputs
cards.body: Fixed inputs never change. Dynamic inputs are built on the spot to match the moment.

left.text: Always searches 'news' no matter what you asked

right.text: Searches 'Mars rover 2024' because that's your question now

exercises.prompt: A user says: 'Delete the file report_old.txt.' The agent picks a delete tool. What should it bind to the 'path' parameter?
exercises.explain: Dynamic binding means the agent reads the current request and fills 'path' with the exact file the user named: report_old.txt.

exercises.prompt: True or False: With dynamic parameter binding, the agent generates argument values based on the current situation.
exercises.explain: That's exactly the idea. The model builds fresh inputs each time to match what you asked and what's happening right now.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about dynamic binding.
exercises.sentence: The agent fills a tool's inputs with values based on the current situation.
exercises.explain: Dynamic binding uses the CURRENT situation to decide what values to write into the tool's inputs.

exercises.prompt: You ask an agent to 'find flights to Rome.' Which tool call was generated by the AI using dynamic parameter binding?
exercises.explain: The AI reads your request and binds the destination to 'Rome', the value that actually matches what you asked for.

exercises.prompt: An agent has a 'run_code' tool. A user says: 'Add up the numbers in sales.csv.' What must the agent do to succeed?

options.text: Pick run_code AND generate code that reads sales.csv and sums the numbers
Pick run_code but leave the code input blank
Use the same fixed code it always runs
Refuse because the file name changes

exercises.explain: Choosing the right tool is only half the job. The agent must also bind good argument values, here, generating code that targets sales.csv based on the current request. That two-step skill, select the tool then fill its inputs from the live situation, is what makes an agent genuinely useful.
